data.title: Mercantile Bank forecasts 5% to 7% annualized quarterly loan growth in 2026 while expecting net interest margin to rise in the back half
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Mercantile Bank Corporation (MBWM) Q1 2026

Management View

  • “Our results for the first quarter of 2026 continue to build on the theme of commercial expertise generating a strong return profile,” said “the consummation of the purchase of Eastern Michigan on December 31, 2025, represents execution of our strategic objectives around deposit growth, loan growth and margin stability, paired with strong asset quality and overall financial performance” (President, CEO & Director Raymond Reitsma).
  • Reitsma highlighted margin and balance-sheet positioning, saying, “Over the last 5 quarters, the SOFR 90-day average rate has dropped 67 basis points, while our margin increased by 8 basis points to 3.55%,” and added, “this illustrates effective execution of our strategic objective to maintain a steady margin by matched funding of our assets and liabilities” (President, CEO & Director Reitsma).
  • On credit and liquidity, Reitsma said, “Non-performing assets to total assets remain at the low levels typical of our company at 11 basis points of total assets as of March 31, 2026,” and “the allowance for credit losses stands at 1.18% of total loans as of March 31, 2026,” while “our loan-to-deposit ratio stood at 89% compared to 91% on December 31, 2025” (President, CEO & Director Reitsma).
  • Christmas reported earnings and acquisition-adjusted results: “We announced net income of $22.7 million or $1.32 per diluted share for the first quarter of 2026,” and “excluding after-tax one-time costs associated with the year-end 2025 acquisition of Eastern Michigan and previously announced core and digital banking system conversion, net income improved to $25.2 million or $1.46 per diluted share” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Charles Christmas).

Outlook

  • “This forecast is predicated on no changes in the federal funds rate during the remainder of 2026,” and “we are projecting loan growth in a range of 5% to 7% annualized during each quarter” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).
  • Christmas guided near-term margin direction and second-half improvement: “We are forecasting our second quarter net interest margin to be similar to that of the first quarter with steady increases throughout the last half of the year” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).
  • In Q&A, Christmas tied a tempered margin range to mix, saying, “that is a reflection of the change in our balance sheet mix,” and “all of that deposit growth went to the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).

Financial Results

  • Christmas said, “Net interest income increased $7.4 million during the first quarter of 2026 compared to the prior year first quarter,” and “the net interest margin was 3.55% during the first quarter of 2026 compared to 3.47% during the first quarter of 2025” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).
  • On credit provisioning, Christmas said, “We recorded a negative provision expense of $1.8 million during the first quarter of 2026,” and attributed it primarily to “improved economic forecast, changes in loan mix, a reduction in the residential mortgage loan portfolio, a decline in specific allocations and limited net growth in commercial loans due to the significant volume of loan payoff and partial paydowns” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).
  • Reitsma cited operating leverage and Eastern Michigan impacts on expense line items, saying net revenue “grew 18.1% to $67.6 million,” while “other expenses include a $1.2 million increase in allocations to reserve for unfunded loan commitments” and “a $0.9 million increase in the core deposit intangible asset amortization account arising from the acquisition of Eastern Michigan” (President, CEO & Director Reitsma).

Q&A

  • Brendan Nosal, Hovde Group: “this quarter came in towards the lower end of the guided range… you tempered the range for the remainder of the year” and asked for the drivers; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas: “the bottom line is just more deposits, same level of loans… those more deposit balances are going into the lower-yielding account at the Federal Reserve.”
  • Damon Del Monte, KBW: asked about fair value accretion in margin and whether payoffs were tied to Eastern Michigan; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas: “it’s only about a 1 basis point impact to our margin,” and President, CEO & Director Reitsma: “No, it was entirely the former, not the latter.”
  • Nathan Race, Piper Sandler: asked about excess liquidity targets and deposit costs; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas: “our securities, we’re right around 16% of total assets now and the plan is to keep it there,” and on cash, “I would expect the balance to be well over $200 million at the end of the year.”
  • Daniel Tamayo, Raymond James: pressed downside risk if payoffs stay elevated and buyback flexibility; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas: “the surprise would be… the payoff side,” and on margin impact, “maybe 2 to 5 basis points of margin compression below what the guidance is,” while on buybacks, “it’s always on the table.”
  • Matthew Breese, Stephens: asked about securities maturities and competition; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas: “There’s $50 million maturing this year yet… $100 million maturing every year for the next 5 years,” and on deposits, “deposit rates have been very, very quiet,” while President, CEO & Director Reitsma said loan competition “really hasn’t changed for some time.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was slightly negative to neutral on the key swing factors, with repeated probing on “tempered” margin expectations, payoff persistence, liquidity deployment, and capital return (Nosal, Race, Tamayo).
  • Management tone was slightly positive in prepared remarks and more explanatory in Q&A, including “we are very pleased with our operating results” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas) while also qualifying forecasting with “market conditions remain volatile making forecasting difficult” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).
  • Compared with the prior quarter’s emphasis on acquisition mechanics and initial 2026 setup, the current call featured more back-and-forth on mix-driven margin pressure from excess cash at the Fed and the timing of payoff normalization; in Q4 2025, management said 2026 loan growth would include “expected meaningful payoffs over the next several months” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Margin framing shifted from Q4 2025’s expectation that “our first quarter 2026 net interest margin [would] increase from the fourth quarter of 2025” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas) to Q1 2026’s explanation that heavy deposit growth and muted loan growth pushed funds to the Fed, with Christmas saying the updated margin view reflects “starting at a lower spot” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).
  • Analysts stayed focused on margin and loan growth in both quarters, but Q1 2026 included more direct questioning on downside cases and capital actions, while Q4 2025 included more acquisition-related questions on Eastern liquidity deployment and purchase accounting.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged forecasting uncertainty: “market conditions remain volatile making forecasting difficult” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).
  • The primary operating risk discussed was payoff-driven loan growth variability and its margin impact, with Christmas warning that if elevated payoffs persist, “we would end up with a higher level of money sitting at the Federal Reserve… [which] would put a damper some compression on our margin” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Christmas).

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that Q1 2026 results reflected Eastern Michigan’s addition, strong deposit growth, and “very strong asset quality,” while acknowledging that elevated payoffs and the resulting build in lower-yielding Fed balances were key near-term drivers behind a lower starting point for margin progression. For the rest of 2026, management’s core setup centered on a “no changes” rate assumption, projected “5% to 7% annualized” quarterly loan growth, and a second-half margin lift tied to balance-sheet repositioning and loan growth, alongside continued personnel investment and an ongoing core/digital banking conversion timeline.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Steel Dynamics surges to all-time high on rising Q1 profit, positive outlook
data.description:
Steel Dynamics (STLD) up 5.7% in Tuesday's trading to an all-time intraday high $223.78 after reporting Q1 earnings that narrowly missed expectations but nearly doubled from a year ago and offering an upbeat outlook for American steel demand.

Q1 net profit jumped to $403.4M, or $2.78/share, from $217.2M, or $1.44/share, in the year-earlier quarter, net sales rose to $5.2B from $4.37B a year ago, and steel shipments reached an all-time quarterly high of 3.6M tons.

"We are seeing an improved steel market environment, supported by domestic trade actions, manufacturing onshoring, infrastructure program funding, and the increasing regionalization of supply chains in the United States," CEO Mark Millett said.

"Underlying steel demand strengthened during the first quarter 2026, as customer orders rebounded and backlogs increased across our steel and steel fabrication operations," the CEO said. "Steel prices continued to improve, and lead times extended."

Steel Dynamics (STLD) does not issue guidance this early in the quarter, but positive commentary on its earnings conference call likely is helping to push shares higher.

The company said flat-rolled steel market conditions continue to improve, supported by strong demand and lower imports, and customers remain optimistic about the outlook; it also sees improving value-added spreads in the flat-rolled segment.

Steel Dynamics (STLD) said long product steel markets remain strong in 2026, and it expects another solid year as demand and pricing remain favorable, particularly in structural steel and railroad rail.

North American automotive production estimates for 2026 are expected to come in similar to 2025, the company said, noting it has become a supplier of choice for many U.S.-based European and Asian automotive producers, due in part to its lower carbon content capabilities.

Also, non-residential construction remains strong, led by data centers and an increase in multi-family homebuilding, and oil and gas activity has been strong, with pipe mills already booked well into the summer, the company said.

data.title: Weekly ETF flows: two of 11 sectors record outflows; Gold leads inflows
data.description:
The world's largest exchange-traded fund, SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY), saw inflows of $3.5B for the week ending April 17, while its price increased by 3.5%.

The SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD) recorded inflows totaling $1.93B last week as GLD prices increased 2.43% during the week. iShares Silver Trust ETF (SLV) also recorded inflows totaling $20.93M, while its price jumped over 7.8% during the week. The iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) also registered inflows of $906.09M last week, while the Bitcoin (BTC-USD) price increased 2.10% over the same period.

Last week’s inflows/outflows

The 11 S&P 500 sector tracking ETFs collectively recorded inflows of about $3.11B last week, according to data from etfdb.com.

The highest inflows last week were seen in the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE), totaling $1.14B, followed by the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) with inflows of $875.53M. The Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) recorded an inflow of $706.48M last week.

Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU) led sector outflows, as two out of 11 sectors saw money flowing out of their respective sector-wise funds. The Utilities Sector (XLU) saw an outflow of $539.04M, followed by Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) with $134.52M flowing out last week.

Breakdown of S&P 500 sector fund flows:

data.title: T-Mobile US gains on report Deutsche Telekom evaluating combination
data.description:
T-Mobile US (TMUS) rose 1.5% after a report that Deutsche Telekom AG (DTEGY) is evaluating a full combination with its American arm.

The German carrier has been discussing creating a new holding company that would make a stock bid for shares of both Deutsche Telekom and T-Mobile (TMUS), according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Deutsche Telekom already has a 53% stake in T-Mobile.

The combined company, which would be jointly owned by the companies' current investors, may look to list in the US and a major European exchange, according to the report. 

Deliberations are said to be at a preliminary stage and any deal would require political support to move forward, according to the report. 

“As per their usual practice, DT and TMUS do not comment on speculation regarding their corporate activity, nor are there specifics for us to comment on for your inquiry,” a spokesperson for Deutsche Telekom said in an emailed statement to Bloomberg. T-Mobile declined to comment. 

T-Mobile (TMUS) is set to report Q1 results next Tuesday.

data.title: Genuine Parts is still on track for a 2027 business separation
data.description:
Genuine Parts Company (GPC) reiterated on Tuesday that it is still on track to separate into two independent, publicly traded companies in 2027.

The planned transaction will carve the company into a dedicated global automotive business, centered on its NAPA-branded automotive parts distribution network, and a separate global industrial business built around its motion industrial parts and services operations. The split is intended to be tax‑free for U.S. shareholders and is expected to create two "industry‑leading" companies with sharper strategic focus, simplified operations, and the ability to pursue tailored capital allocation and investment agendas. 

The board of directors of Genuine Parts (GPC) does not need shareholder approval to complete the separation, which remains subject to customary conditions, including SEC review of a Form 10. Details on names, boards, and executive teams will come later.

Alongside its first-quarter earnings report, Genuine Parts (GPC) reaffirmed its full‑year 2026 guidance. Management described the upcoming separation as a continuation of a multi-year portfolio simplification and growth strategy designed to enhance long‑term shareholder value across both the automotive and industrial franchises.

Shares of Genuine Parts (GPC) are down more than 20% since tension in the Middle East disrupted the auto supplier sector.

data.title: Lithia Motors Q1 2026 dividend preview: Will April extend 15-year growth streak?
data.description:
Lithia Motors (LAD) is expected to announce a dividend increase this April, continuing its 15-year streak of continuous dividend growth.

Based on past patterns, analysts expect a consensus annual dividend of ~$2.26 per share, which brings the quarterly dividend to ~$0.565 per share. This clearly implies an increase of ~2.7% from the current ~$0.55 dividend payout.

The company last paid a dividend of ~$0.55 per share in January 2026, which comes to an annual yield of ~0.76%. However, earlier, in April last year, it raised the dividend by ~3.8%, from ~$0.53 to ~$0.55.

Over the past five years, the company has delivered a dividend growth rate of ~12.33% and currently maintains a payout ratio of ~6.44%.

Moreover, as per dividend metrics ratings, the company carries an A+ for safety, a C+ for growth, a D+ for yield, and a B+ for consistency.

Now, investors should keep a close watch on the Q1 2026 results, which will be released on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, before the market opens.

data.title: AI is emerging as a productivity engine, not a job killer - Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AIQ) (AIEQ) is driving meaningful gains in U.S. labor productivity—but not through the job displacement many economists feared, according to a new analysis from Morgan Stanley Research released Tuesday.

According to analyst Katy Huberty, industries with high AI exposure contributed 1.7 percentage points of the 2.4 percentage point growth in output per employee across the U.S. in 2025. That marks a significant acceleration from just 0.7 percentage points in 2024.

The more surprising finding: these productivity gains are coming from faster output growth rather than workforce reductions.

“Trends in employment growth have been similar across industries with different degrees of AI exposure, while output growth has accelerated more rapidly in industries that we have identified as having higher AI exposure,” Huberty wrote in the note.

Tech-oriented sectors have led the charge, posting the strongest acceleration in both output and labor productivity. The dynamic suggests companies are using AI tools to produce more with their existing workforce rather than trimming headcount to achieve efficiency gains, offering early evidence that AI adoption may follow a more benign path than previous waves of automation, at least in the near term.

data.title: No significant changes expected with new Fed chair – Wells Fargo’s Paul Christopher
data.description:
Paul Christopher, head of global investment strategy at Wells Fargo, said he does not expect significant changes to monetary policy even amid potential leadership shifts at the Federal Reserve.

“We…wouldn’t expect markets to be riled,” Christopher said in an interview with CNBC.

The strategist emphasized that the institutional structure of the Federal Reserve serves as a safeguard against dramatic policy changes. Christopher explained that any major shift would require convincing nearly two dozen officials, noting there are “18 other people that need to be persuaded, plus the staff, and it just doesn’t seem like that’s a task that anyone really wants to try to take on.”

Turning to energy markets, Christopher predicted that the sharp rebound in WTI crude prices (CL1:COM) since late March will inevitably unwind.

“I’m not saying that WTI is going to $120 or $150,” he clarified, but added that “markets have been trading on momentum here, and the momentum has been following headlines.”

Christopher cautioned investors against reacting too strongly to day-to-day diplomatic developments and geopolitical news.

He described the current market environment as being on a “treadmill of following headlines too closely and extrapolating from them,” urging investors instead to focus on what matters most: “earnings and valuations.”

Wells Fargo’s current guidance recommends that investors consider trimming energy positions in favor of technology stocks (VGT), (XLK), (IYW). Christopher noted that the information technology sector “has underperformed energy (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE) over the past several weeks,” adding that the fundamentals do not fully support energy’s recent outperformance.

Rather than attempting to pick individual AI winners, Christopher advised taking broad exposure to the information technology sector.

He highlighted that tech valuations have become more attractive, pointing out that “you’re not paying anymore a premium for tech over the rest of the index.”

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT).

data.title: Invesco dividend preview: April raise seen extending 4-year run ahead of Q1 results
data.description:
Invesco (IVZ) is anticipated to announce a dividend increase this April, extending its 4-year streak of continuous dividend growth.

Based on historical charts and patterns, analysts expect a consensus annual dividend of $0.88 per share, implying a quarterly dividend of ~$0.22, which would represent a ~4.8% increase from the current $0.21 distribution.

The company last declared a dividend of $0.21 per share in January 2026, representing an annual yield of 3.37%, and raised its dividend last April by ~2.4% to $0.21 from $0.2050.

The investment management company has delivered a 5-year dividend growth rate of ~6.26% and maintains an average payout ratio of ~41.38%.

Moreover, as far as dividend metrics are concerned, the company holds ratings of C+ for safety, D+ for growth, B for yield, and B for dividend consistency.

Invesco (IVZ) is all set to report its Q1 2026 results on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, before the market opens.
